description: Analyze the equity and land-use implications of exclusionary single-family zoning in San Francisco by creating a reproducible Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/exclusionary_zoning.ipynb. Using the 'buildings', 'parcels', and 'households' data from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5, identify single-family dominated zones (sf_pct > 0.80) and compare them to diverse zones (sf_pct <= 0.80) based on median household income. The analysis must identify the appropriate building_type_id for single-family homes, calculate zone-level metrics (filtering for zones with at least 50 residential units and valid income data), and determine the Pearson correlation between single-family housing percentage and income. Export a zone-level dataset to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/exclusionary_zoning_metrics.csv including zone_id, total_res_units, sf_units, sf_pct, median_income, and zoning_category. Generate a summary JSON at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zoning_equity_summary.json with keys 'exclusive_sf_zone_count', 'diverse_zone_count', 'exclusive_sf_median_income', 'diverse_median_income', and 'correlation_sf_pct_income'. Save a scatter plot of sf_pct against median_income to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/sf_income_scatter.png. Ensure the notebook is fully executed.
